14.reason: Error: fabs returns a double, and the condition should check that a[i+1] > a[i-1], not just absolute difference >= 1. Also, when i=0, a[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: fabs returns a double, and the condition should check that a[i+1] > a[i-1], not just absolute difference >= 1. Also, when i=0, a[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: fabs() returns a double, and the condition should check that a[i+1] > a[i-1], not just absolute difference >= 1. Also, when i=0, a[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: no bounds check (i-1 or i+1 may be out of range); condition should be a[i+1] > a[i-1] instead of fabs(...)>=1; also fabs returns double, not appropriate
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Should use a[i+1] > a[i-1] instead of fabs (which allows decreasing order)
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: fabs allows reverse order expansion (e.g., d-a); should check a[i+1] > a[i-1]. Also accesses a[-1] when i=0 causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  fabs calculates absolute difference, failing to verify that a[i+1] > a[i-1] as required; should use a[i+1] > a[i-1].
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Using 'fabs' from math.h for integer comparison is unnecessary and incorrect; also, condition should ensure a[i+1] > a[i-1], not just absolute difference >=1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Using 'fabs' from <math.h> on integers is unnecessary and may cause type mismatch; also, condition should ensure a[i+1] > a[i-1], not just absolute difference >=1]
